The device includes a 2MP main camera, Dual SIM support, and is powered by a removable 1300 mAh battery.

It features premium materials including an 18k gold bezel and crown, sapphire crystal, and a zircon-based amorphous alloy case. The device boasts a 1.5-inch LTPO AMOLED display, runs HarmonyOS 4.0, and is highly durable with IP68 and 10 ATM water resistance (suitable for diving). Key technical specs include a 530 mAh battery with wireless charging, comprehensive health sensors (ECG, SpO2, body temperature), and multi-system GPS, though it lacks cellular connectivity.

It is powered by a Mediatek Helio P60 chipset with options for 4GB or 6GB of RAM and 64GB or 128GB of storage. The device includes a 16MP dual main camera, a 16MP selfie camera, and a 3000 mAh battery supporting wireless charging. Notable features include Infrared Face ID and an infrared port, though it lacks a 3.5mm headphone jack.

entry.lex: The AT&T Quickfire is a discontinued QWERTY-keyboard phone released in November 2008. It features a 2.8-inch TFT display, a 1.3 MP main camera, and limited internal memory of 29MB, expandable via microSD. Connectivity is basic, supporting GSM/HSPA networks and Bluetooth 1.2, but lacking WLAN and a 3.5mm jack. It is powered by a removable 1200 mAh battery.

The Fairphone 4 is a 5G-enabled smartphone released in October 2021, featuring a 6.3-inch IPS LCD display and a Snapdragon 750G 5G chipset. It offers memory configurations of 128GB/6GB or 256GB/8GB RAM and is equipped with a dual 48MP main camera system and a 25MP selfie camera. Notable for its durability and sustainability, it is MIL-STD-810G compliant, IP54 rated, and includes a removable 3905 mAh battery and a standard 5-year warranty.

It offers storage options up to 512GB and a versatile dual-camera system headlined by a 48MP wide lens. The device is IP68 water-resistant, uses a USB Type-C port, and is powered by a 4383 mAh battery supporting MagSafe wireless charging. It is available in Black, Blue, Green, Yellow, and Pink, with a starting price of approximately $929.99.

For photography, it has a 13MP dual rear camera and a 5MP selfie camera, both supporting 1080p video. The device is powered by a 3000 mAh battery and includes a rear-mounted fingerprint sensor, FM radio, and a 3.5mm headphone jack.

It is powered by a 1.4 GHz Snapdragon S2 chipset with 512MB RAM and 1GB internal storage (expandable via microSDHC). Key features include an 8MP main camera with 720p video recording, a removable 1500 mAh battery, and an HDMI port, though it lacks a selfie camera.

Released in November 2023, the Honor 100 is a 5G smartphone featuring a high-brightness 6.7-inch 120Hz OLED display (2600 nits peak). It is powered by the Snapdragon 7 Gen 3 chipset with up to 16GB RAM and 512GB storage. The device boasts a high-resolution 50MP main camera with OIS and a 50MP selfie camera, both capable of 4K video. It is equipped with a 5000mAh battery supporting fast 100W wired charging and is priced at approximately 320 EUR.

It comes with 8GB of RAM and storage options of 128GB or 256GB. Notable highlights include a massive 6000 mAh battery with 45W fast charging, a 50MP main camera, and stereo speakers, all priced at ₹11,999.

This compact silver device features a 2.2-inch TFT display, a 3.15 MP main camera, and a removable 1000mAh Li-Ion battery providing up to 3 hours of talk time. It includes basic connectivity like Bluetooth and FM radio, lacks a selfie camera and 3.5mm jack, and supports microSD expansion.

It is equipped with 128GB storage, 6GB RAM, and a long-lasting 5000mAh battery with 18W wired charging. The device features a triple rear camera system led by a 50MP wide lens and an 8MP selfie camera, both supporting 1080p video.

It is powered by a Snapdragon 625 chipset with options for 3GB or 4GB of RAM and 32GB or 64GB of storage. Key hardware includes a 12MP main camera capable of 4K video, an 8MP selfie camera, and a 3500 mAh battery with 18W fast charging. It originally launched with Android 7.1 (upgradable to 8.1) and was priced around 290 EUR.

It features a 5.7-inch IPS LCD display, 32GB internal storage with 2GB RAM, and a quad-core 1.3 GHz processor. The device includes a 5MP dual main camera, a 2MP selfie camera, and is powered by a removable 2700 mAh battery.

originaltripleset.otriple: Oukitel RT6 | has_feature | reverse wired charging

modifiedtripleset.mtriple: Oukitel RT6 | has_feature | reverse wired charging

entry.lex: The Oukitel RT6 is a rugged 10.1-inch Android 13 tablet released in July 2023, priced around 350 EUR. It is built for extreme durability with IP68/IP69K ratings, MIL-STD-810H compliance, and drop resistance. Its standout feature is a massive 20,000 mAh battery with 33W fast charging and reverse wired charging. Performance is driven by a Mediatek MT8788 chipset with 8GB RAM and 256GB UFS 2.1 storage. It features 16MP front and rear cameras and weighs 1030g.

originaltripleset.otriple: LeEco Le 2 | chipset | Helio X20 or Snapdragon 652

modifiedtripleset.mtriple: LeEco Le 2 | chipset | Helio X20 or Snapdragon 652

entry.lex: The LeEco Le 2 is a discontinued Android 6.0 smartphone released in April 2016. It features a 5.5-inch IPS LCD display (1080 x 1920), 3GB of RAM with either 32GB or 64GB of internal storage, and is powered by either a Helio X20 or Snapdragon 652 chipset. Key hardware includes a 16MP main camera capable of 4K video, an 8MP selfie camera, and a 3000 mAh battery with 18W fast charging. Notable connectivity includes USB Type-C and an infrared port, though it lacks a 3.5mm headphone jack and NFC.

entry.lex: The Kyocera Hydro Life is a discontinued rugged smartphone released in August 2014. It is highly durable, featuring IP57 dust/water resistance and MIL-STD-810G compliance. The device has a 4.5-inch IPS LCD (540x960), runs Android 4.3 on a Snapdragon 200 quad-core processor, and includes 8GB of storage with 1.5GB of RAM. It is equipped with a 5MP main camera, a 2MP selfie camera, and a removable 2000 mAh battery.

The Icemobile Prime 5.5 is a discontinued Android 4.4.2 (KitKat) smartphone released in August 2014. It features a 5.46-inch IPS LCD display (720x1280), a quad-core 1.3 GHz CPU, 1GB of RAM, and 16GB of internal storage. It is equipped with an 8MP main camera, a 2MP selfie camera, and a removable 2250 mAh battery.

originaltripleset.otriple: Blackview A96 | chipset | Mediatek Helio G99

modifiedtripleset.mtriple: Blackview A96 | chipset | Mediatek Helio G99

entry.lex: Released in September 2023, the Blackview A96 is a budget-friendly smartphone priced at approximately $161.69. It features a 6.5-inch 120Hz IPS LCD display and is powered by a Mediatek Helio G99 chipset with options for 8GB or 12GB of RAM and 256GB of UFS 2.2 storage. The device includes a dual rear camera setup led by a 48MP wide lens and a 16MP selfie camera. It runs Android 13 and is supported by a 4380 mAh battery with 18W wired charging.

Its standout feature is a massive 7000 mAh battery, paired with a large 6.8-inch IPS LCD display. It is powered by a Unisoc SC9863A octa-core chipset with 2GB RAM and 32GB of internal storage. The device includes a triple main camera setup led by a 13MP sensor and a 5MP selfie camera.

entry.lex: The Ericsson A2628 is a discontinued GSM phone released in 2001. It features a basic monochrome graphic display with 4 lines of resolution, no camera, and no modern connectivity like Bluetooth or WLAN. Key features include SMS messaging, WAP 1.1, and three built-in games. It is powered by a removable NiMH battery, with high-capacity options extending talk time up to 11 hours.

entry.lex: The Meizu 18s Pro is a 5G smartphone released in September 2021, featuring a high-resolution 6.7-inch Super AMOLED display with a 120Hz refresh rate and Gorilla Glass 6 protection. It is powered by the Snapdragon 888+ 5G chipset, offering memory options up to 12GB RAM and 256GB UFS 3.1 storage. The device boasts a triple main camera system led by a 50MP wide sensor with OIS and 8K video capabilities, complemented by a 44MP selfie camera. It includes a 4500 mAh battery supporting 40W wired and wireless charging, an ultrasonic under-display fingerprint sensor, and stereo speakers, priced at approximately 610 EUR.

It features a 5.0-inch IPS LCD display, a dual-core 1.3 GHz processor, and limited memory with 512MB RAM and 4GB internal storage. For photography, it has an 8MP main camera and a 2MP selfie camera. The device is powered by a 2000 mAh battery and supports dual SIM (GSM) connectivity.

entry.lex: The VK Mobile VK300 is a discontinued GSM phone released in July 2005. It is a compact, lightweight device (75g) featuring a 1.8-inch TFT display and a secondary external STN display. Lacking a camera, Bluetooth, WLAN, and USB, it provides basic functionality including SMS/MMS, a WAP 1.2.1 browser, and a removable 720 mAh battery offering up to 3 hours of talk time.
